title: M5/SQL: wrong result for range predicate
poc: create table test (pre integer not null primary key, size integer not null, level smallint not null, kind smallint not null, prop char (32));
SELECT c0002.* FROM test AS c0002, test AS c0001 WHERE c0001.pre + c0001.size >= c0002.pre AND c0002.pre > c0001.pre AND c0001.prop = 'auctionG.xml';
SELECT c0002.* FROM test AS c0002, test AS c0001 WHERE c0002.pre > c0001.pre AND c0001.prop = 'auctionG.xml';
select distinct c2.pre from test as c2, test as c1 where c1.kind = 6 and c1.prop = 'auctionG.xml' and c2.kind = 1 and c2.pre > c1.pre and c1.pre + c1.size >= c2.pre;
key_bug_features: Incorrect results for range queries with specific conditions
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test (pre integer NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, size integer NOT NULL, level smallint NOT NULL, kind smallint NOT NULL, prop char(32));
INSERT INTO test (pre, size, level, kind, prop) VALUES (1, 10, 1, 1, 'auctionG.xml');
INSERT INTO test (pre, size, level, kind, prop) VALUES (2, 5, 2, 6, 'auctionG.xml');
INSERT INTO test (pre, size, level, kind, prop) VALUES (3, 15, 3, 1, NULL);
SELECT c0002.* FROM test AS c0002, test AS c0001 WHERE c0001.pre + c0001.size >= c0002.pre AND c0002.pre > c0001.pre AND c0001.prop IS NOT NULL;
